---
title: "Keep heterogeneous features on native grids"
subtitle: "Record stimulus and scan clocks without upsampling before modeling."
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Use when

You have feature families sampled at different rates and need lossless timing
bookkeeping before an fMRI model is built.

This example describes a 0–200 s scan with TR 1.77 s. Stimulus time 30 s maps to
scan time 7 s. The TR is intentionally not passed to any feature constructor:
it belongs to the downstream model.

```{python}
import numpy as np

from natural_features import (
    ClockMap,
    EventSeries,
    FeatureBundle,
    FeatureSeries,
    SupportSpec,
    TemporalContext,
    TimebaseSpec,
)

metadata = lambda name: {"extractor_id": name, "params_hash": "demo"}
context = TemporalContext(
    (ClockMap("stimulus", "scan:run-01", offset_s=-23.0),)
)

fast_times = 30.0 + np.arange(6) * 0.1
fast = FeatureSeries(
    values=np.arange(6, dtype=np.float32)[:, None],
    times_s=fast_times,
    metadata=metadata("demo.fast"),
    timebase=TimebaseSpec(
        kind="audio_hop",
        reference="stimulus",
        hop_s=0.1,
        support=SupportSpec(kind="window", width_s=0.1),
    ),
)

slow_times = 30.0 + np.arange(4) * 0.5
slow = FeatureSeries(
    values=np.arange(4, dtype=np.float32)[:, None],
    times_s=slow_times,
    metadata=metadata("demo.slow"),
    timebase=TimebaseSpec(
        kind="windows",
        reference="stimulus",
        stride_s=0.5,
        support=SupportSpec(kind="window", width_s=0.5),
    ),
)

words = EventSeries(
    onset_s=np.array([30.05, 30.62, 31.41]),
    offset_s=np.array([30.31, 30.94, 31.78]),
    label=np.array(["the", "room", "opens"], dtype=object),
    metadata=metadata("demo.words"),
    timebase=TimebaseSpec(kind="events", reference="stimulus"),
)

bundle = FeatureBundle(
    {"fast": fast, "slow": slow, "words": words},
    temporal_context=context,
    metadata={"scan_duration_s": 200.0},
)
```

## Convert coordinates, not sampling density

```{python}
fast_scan = bundle.in_clock("fast", "scan:run-01")
slow_scan = bundle.in_clock("slow", "scan:run-01")

{
    "fast_rows": len(fast_scan.times_s),
    "slow_rows": len(slow_scan.times_s),
    "stimulus_30_is_scan": context.convert(
        30.0,
        source="stimulus",
        target="scan:run-01",
    ),
    "fast_values_reused": fast_scan.values is fast.values,
}
```

The 100 ms and 500 ms features retain six and four rows. There is no finest-grid
upsampling step and no silent interpolation.

## Hand one native feature downstream

```{python}
payload = bundle.temporal_payload("words", target_clock="scan:run-01")

{
    "object_type": payload.object_type,
    "clock": str(payload.clock),
    "onset_s": payload.onset_s.tolist(),
    "offset_s": payload.offset_s.tolist(),
    "has_tr": "tr_s" in payload.timebase,
}
```

The payload contains native values or event bounds, its clock, support, schema,
metadata, and all known clock mappings. A downstream library can then choose a
TR, interpolation rule, HRF, lags, and design-matrix policy explicitly.

## Refuses to

- Treat feature row number as time.
- Upsample all features to the finest observed grid.
- Merge different clocks into a wide table without a target clock and join
  policy.
- Store TR or HRF choices as if they were native feature metadata.

## See also

[Native temporal contract](../spec/native-temporal-contract.qmd) · [Table interchange](../concepts/table-interchange.qmd)
